parameters.name: kinds
parameters.description:
Comma-separated list of kinds to include. Defaults to all.
Allowed: `audit`, `reading` (Server stream). `scan`/`health` were
moved to the Library stream (M13) and are silently ignored here.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: RFC 3339 timestamp; only entries strictly newer than this are returned.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description:
Path to list. Omit (or pass empty) to list the root. The server
will reject anything that doesn't canonicalise to a descendant of
`COMIC_LIBRARY_PATH`.

403.description: admin only, or path is outside library root

parameters.name: library_id
parameters.description:
Restrict to log entries whose `fields["library_id"]` matches
this UUID. Populated by the scanner's instrumented spans
(`scan_library`, `scan_series_folder`) — every event emitted
under those spans inherits the library_id via the
`RingLayer::on_event` parent-span walk. Omit / `all` for
cross-library.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description:
Stream filter (observability-split M12): `server` (app runtime, the
Server-log default) | `library` (scanner/worker events, also surfaced
in Library activity) | `all`. Omit ⇒ all.

get.summary:
Series with auto-sync enabled (`metadata_sync_paused = false`) — the
opt-in set the weekly refresh cron will touch. Auto-sync is
series-level, so issues inherit their series' setting. The list is
operator-curated (opt-in), so it's intentionally bounded and returned
in full, ordered by name.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description:
ISO-8601 timestamp; returns rows older than this for
cursor-style pagination.

get.summary:
Most-recent scan_run row per library. One row per library, ordered
oldest-scanned-first so the "What hasn't been touched in months"
libraries float to the top — drives the dashboard's "Latest scan
per library" card.

post.summary:
Destructive: wipe one issue's strip subtree, then enqueue a fresh
`Strip` job. Cover file is preserved. Audit:
`admin.thumbnails.force_recreate.issue_page_map`.

Fill-only `Strip` enqueue for a single issue. Worker skips pages whose
strip files already exist, so calling this when nothing's missing is a
no-op. Audit: `admin.thumbnails.generate_page_map.issue`.

Wipe one issue's cover thumbnail (every known extension), clear its
stamp columns, and enqueue a fresh `Cover` job. Strip subtree is
preserved. Audit: `admin.thumbnails.regenerate.issue_cover`.

Destructive: delete every strip subtree in the series, then enqueue a
`Strip` job per active issue so each page is re-encoded from the
archive. Cover files are preserved. Audit:
`admin.thumbnails.force_recreate.series_page_map`.

Fill-only enqueue: walk every active issue in the series and push a
`Strip` job. The worker skips pages whose strip files already exist, so
repeated calls are cheap and idempotent. Audit:
`admin.thumbnails.generate_page_map.series`.

Wipe every cover file in a series and re-enqueue cover jobs for each
active issue. Strip subtree is preserved; strips refill lazily through
the inline-fallback path or by a separate `force-recreate-page-map`
call. Audit: `admin.thumbnails.regenerate.series_cover`.

post.description:
Always returns 204 — the response doesn't leak whether the email is on
file (otherwise the endpoint doubles as a user-enumeration oracle).
When the email maps to an active local account, a 1-hour reset token
is sent via the configured EmailSender.

204.description: request accepted (whether or not the email exists)

post.description:
Always 204 (no enumeration). Sends a fresh 24h token only when the
account exists, is local, and is `pending_verification`.

204.description: request accepted (whether or not the email exists)

post.description:
Verifies the HMAC token, rehashes the new password, bumps
`token_version` (so every existing session for the user is
invalidated), and sends a confirmation email. Returns 204 on success.

204.description: password reset; all other sessions revoked

get.description:
Consumes a 24h verification token; on success flips
`state=pending_verification` → `active` and `email_verified=true`, then
302s to `/sign-in?verified=1`. Re-clicking a still-valid token after
activation is a 302 to the same target (idempotent / harmless).

302.description: RP-initiated logout — caller is redirected to the OIDC issuer's end_session_endpoint

Resolve the next issue the user should read after the given one. Picks
CBL > series; returns `source: "none"` when neither branch has a
viable target.

parameters.description: Saved-view id (kind='cbl') the caller is reading through

Resolve the previous issue the user should navigate to. Picks
CBL > series; returns `source: "none"` when neither branch has a
viable target (e.g., user is on the first issue of the series and
no CBL context).

parameters.name: cbl
parameters.description: Saved-view id (kind='cbl') the caller is reading through

post.description:
M6 one-click action paired with the synthesized `MetadataDriftFromXml`
health row. Walks every drifted series in the library, composes the
ComicInfo + MetronInfo XML from the current DB state (the composer
reads `field_provenance.set_by='user'` rows and prefers them over the
provider-blank fallback), and enqueues a `RewriteIssueSidecarsJob`
per drifted issue. The series-scope rescan is **not** triggered here
— each per-issue job re-enqueues its own scoped rescan on completion
(same as the issue-scope apply path) — so a partial fan-out failure
doesn't strand the successful issues.

Returns 409 when the library has `metadata_writeback_enabled=false`
(the synth row never appears in that mode either; the 409 protects
scripted callers).

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description:
`unmatched` | `stale` | `all` | `recent` (default `stale`).
`unmatched` is the cheapest scope and the right default for
"I just added a library, get me caught up". `stale` is what
the weekly cron uses. `all` is the operator escape hatch.
`recent` mirrors the Mylar "last N days" window.

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: Bypass mtime fast paths. Defaults to false.

post.summary:
`POST /libraries/{slug}/scan-runs/{scan_id}/cancel` — flip a stuck
scan_runs row to a terminal state. Idempotent against already-
terminal rows (returns 409). The worker is the usual authority on
state transitions; this endpoint is the manual escape hatch for
scans that have lost their worker (e.g., operator cleared the
queue mid-flight, server restart killed an in-flight job, or the
worker hangs and no progress events arrive).
post.description:
Writes `state="cancelled"`, `ended_at=NOW`, `error="Cancelled by
admin"`. Emits `ScanEvent::Failed` so connected WebSocket clients
(the Live scan page) drop the run out of their "active" set
immediately. Race note: if a still-alive worker subsequently
reaches `finalize_run`, it will overwrite this row with its own
terminal state — that's fine and expected.

404.description: library or scan run not found

post.summary:
Tranche C of recovery-visibility — admin-triggered deep page-
decode validation. Walks every active issue in the library,
reads every page entry, runs each through the `image` crate's
decoder, and emits `UnreadablePage` health-issues for failures.
post.description:
Returns immediately. The job runs in a `tokio::spawn` background
task without apalis-style persistence; a server restart abandons
the in-flight run and the operator can re-trigger.

**Cost.** Image-decoding every page in a 20K-issue library can
take 1-2 hours of single-core CPU. Operator-only; never
automatic.

404.description: not found or not owned by caller

get.summary:
Detail endpoint — returns list metadata + aggregate stats only.
Entries used to be embedded here (capped at 500), which silently
truncated long lists; they now live exclusively on the paginated
`/entries` endpoint. The per-status counts the UI used to derive
from `entries` are on `list.stats`.

`GET /me/cbl-lists/{id}/entries` — paginated walk over CBL entries
in position order, hydrated with `IssueSummaryView` for matched
rows. Status filter is server-side, so the Resolution tab can
stream just `ambiguous,missing` without touching the matched majority.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Search imported CBL series, issue, year, or ids

get.summary:
`GET /me/cbl-lists/{id}/export` — round-trip the original `<Books>`
XML back to the user as a `.cbl` download. The bytes are exactly
what we imported (or last refreshed) — every CBL list keeps its
`raw_xml` for re-match purposes, so this is just a Content-Disposition
dressing on top of the stored string.

`GET /me/cbl-lists/{id}/window` — slice of the CBL anchored on the
user's next unfinished matched entry. The window is `before` finished
entries + the current entry + `after` upcoming entries. Used by the
home rail to give a "where am I in this list" view instead of always
starting from position 0.
get.description:
Library-ACL filtering happens at hydrate time: an entry whose issue
lives in a library the user can't see is silently dropped from the
window (same policy as `/issues`). That can leave the window short of
`before + 1 + after` items in heavily-restricted environments; we
don't backfill because filling past the natural cutoff would surface
out-of-order entries to the user.

get.summary:
Bidirectional paginated window over a CBL. Same anchor-aware
initial slice as `/window`, plus `direction=before|after` modes the
home rail uses to lazy-load surrounding entries as the user scrolls
without disturbing the already-rendered anchor band.
get.description:
Library-ACL filtering happens at hydrate time: an entry whose issue
lives in a library the user can't see is silently dropped from the
page (matching `/window`). `min_position` / `max_position` are
computed from the *kept* entries so the cursor returned to the
client never re-fetches an entry it just received.

post.summary:
Multi-select Tranche M3: append many (entry_kind, ref_id) members
to a collection in one round-trip. Per-row insert semantics
match `add_entry` — including the partial-unique idempotent skip,
which the response surfaces as `already_present`. Each member
gets its own light validation + existence check so a single
bad id in the batch can't poison the rest.
post.description:
Position counter walks forward from the collection's current max,
incrementing per accepted insert; concurrent bulk-add races have
the same theoretical position-collision risk as the single-add
endpoint (none enforced at the schema level today), so callers
shouldn't fire two bulk-adds against the same collection in
parallel.

post.summary:
Multi-select Tranche M4: drop many `(entry_kind, ref_id)` members
from a collection in one round-trip. Per-row delete; partial
success preserved. Members not in the collection are silently
counted as `not_present`. The underlying series / issues are NOT
touched — only the collection-membership row.

get.summary:
In-progress issues for the calling user, ordered by most recent
activity. Excludes finished issues, issues that haven't actually been
opened (`last_page = 0`), removed/encrypted issues, issues the user
can't see (library ACL), and issues whose dismissal is still current
(no newer progress write past the dismissal timestamp).
get.description:
Implementation note: dismissals require comparing `progress_records.
updated_at` against `rail_dismissals.dismissed_at`, so we let Postgres
do that in one SQL pass rather than re-filtering rows in Rust.

patch.summary: Bulk-update a per-field patch across a list of issue ids.
patch.description:
One transaction; batched by issue across the patch's fields. ACL
is per-issue via the library access check. Skips per-field +
per-issue when `mode = skip_if_set` AND the column is non-NULL.
Emits one `admin.issue.bulk_metadata_update` audit row per call
with `{ patch_keys, mode, updated_count, requested_count }`.

415.description: page is not a decodable image

get.summary:
"What's next" suggestions for the home page. Returns a mix of
`series_next` cards (next-unread issue in a series the user has read
at least one finished issue of, with no in-progress issue blocking the
queue) and `cbl_next` cards (lowest-position unfinished matched entry
in a CBL the user has any progress in). Series with an active in-
progress issue are skipped — they already surface in Continue Reading.

patch.summary:
`PATCH /me/preferences` — update the calling user's preferences.
Phase 3 only ships `default_reading_direction`. CSRF-checked by middleware
(cookie auth, unsafe verb).

404.description: no such event for this user
no such event for this user

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: opaque cursor from a prior response

post.description: Destructive — deletes ALL of the caller's reading_sessions rows. Audited.

404.description: session not found or not owned by caller

post.summary:
`POST /metadata/batch/{batch_id}/apply` — accept many reviewed candidates
at once. Loops the existing per-entity `ApplySeriesJob`/`ApplyIssueJob`
push (decision matrix, apply mutex, writeback dispatch, audit all
unchanged); only the fan-out is new.
post.description:
`all_strong` / `ordinals` enqueue single-candidate applies. `all_needs_review`
enqueues one **composite** apply per needs-review run (best candidate per
provider merged "most-complete", covers preferring ComicVine) — the bulk
"Fill missing / Replace all" path. The per-request cap + `remainder` then
count runs; re-trigger to drain the rest.

403.description: batch not visible / override_user_edits requires admin

200.description: pin cleared (or no-op when none existed)

get.summary:
Tranche B of recovery-visibility (`~/.claude/plans/recovery-visibility-1.0.md`).
Returns the open `library_health_issues` rows whose payload `path`
matches this issue's file. The issue detail page renders a small
badge when this list is non-empty, and the reader fires a one-time
toast on issue open. Resolved + dismissed rows are excluded — the
badge represents "things you can still act on right now."
get.description:
Auth: regular `CurrentUser` (NOT admin-only). Any user with library
access can see whether the file they're about to read is partial /
recovered. The admin Health tab is still the place to dismiss or
triage.

get.summary:
Returns the next N issues in the same series, ordered by `sort_number`
ASC (NULLS LAST) with `id` as a stable tie-breaker. Removed / soft-deleted
issues are filtered out so the list mirrors the series page. The current
issue is excluded from the result.

Returns the single issue immediately *before* the current one in the same
series — the mirror of [`next_in_series`], using the same ordering
(`sort_number` ASC NULLS LAST, `id` tie-breaker) reversed so the immediate
predecessor is selected. Removed / soft-deleted issues are filtered out.
`item` is `null` when the current issue is the first in the series.

schema.description:
`POST /series/{slug}/metadata/batch` — fan out a per-issue metadata search
over every active issue in the series, grouped under one `metadata_batch`
so progress + review happen in one place. Children run as `manual` (held
for review, never auto-applied).
Which issues a series metadata batch fans out over.

parameters.name: run_id
parameters.description:
Pin a specific run; defaults to the latest run for the
scope/entity.

403.description: library access denied / override requires admin

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: Bypass the size+mtime fast path. Defaults to true.

confirm_password.description:
Optional second password field. The web form ships two `<input>`s
for confirmation; submitting them as `new_password` + `confirm_password`
lets the no-JS fallback validate parity server-side. Ignored when
absent or equal.

current_password.description: Required when changing the password. Verified before any write.

email.description:
New email. Local users only — OIDC users cannot self-edit (the issuer
owns this field).

new_password.description: New password. Must be ≥ 12 chars per the local-auth policy.

source_id.description: Stable identifier within the kind. For aggregates, the bucket time.

ref_id.description:
Series UUID or issue id (TEXT). Validated against the
declared `entry_kind`.

external_url.description:
Optional override; defaults to the canonical URL template for
`(source, entity_type)` when one exists.

AddWidgetReq.description:
Audit-remediation M4 dropped `LogWidgetListView { widgets: Vec<_> }` in
favor of the workspace-uniform `shared::pagination::CursorPage<T>`
envelope (`{ items, next_cursor, total? }`). Widget lists are bounded
per-user; `next_cursor` is always `None`.

config.description:
Optional starting config. Defaults to `{}`, which is always
a legal blob — the per-kind renderer fills in defaults.

next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page. `None` when the result is exhausted.

library_count.description:
Count of `library_user_access` rows for this user. For admins this is
always reported as 0 since admins implicitly see every library.

plaintext.description: The plaintext token. Shown once and never retrievable again.

status.description:
`queued` — the apply job is in flight; the candidate row's
`applied_at` flips once it completes. The Runs / Review-Queue
surfaces (M6) reflect the new state via the same polling
endpoint.

apply_cover.description: Pull + write the cover image. Defaults to true.

cover_overwrite_policy.description:
`never` / `when_missing` (default) / `always`. Only applies to
the primary cover; variants are always additive.

mode.description:
`fill_missing` (default) only writes fields that are currently
empty; `replace_all` overwrites non-user fields. User-set
fields stay sacred regardless unless `override_user_edits=true`
(admin-only).

ordinal.description: 0-based rank from the orchestrator (lower = higher score).

override_external_id_sources.description:
M5 conflict-resolution: per-source list of external-ID rows
where the user has opted to "Use theirs". The candidate's
value replaces the user-set row for these sources. Other
conflicts stay sacred.

override_user_edits.description:
Bypass the user-precedence rule. Admin-only; non-admin callers
get 403 if they request it.

run_id.description:
The run row produced by `POST .../metadata/search`. Required so
the apply job can read the chosen candidate back from
`metadata_run_candidate` rather than re-fetching from the
provider.

selected_fields.description:
M5 preview-pane opt-in: when present, only the named fields
(by `MetadataField::key()`) are applied; everything else is
skipped. When absent, the legacy "apply every eligible field"
behaviour applies (preserves backward compat for older
clients).

actor_label.description:
Human-readable name for the actor (e.g. user display name + email).
`None` when the actor cannot be resolved — typically because the user
row was deleted after the entry was written.

target_label.description:
Human-readable name for the target. Resolved server-side for
`target_type` values of `user` and `library`. `None` for unknown types
or unresolvable rows.

AutoSyncedSeriesRow.description: One auto-synced series row for the admin "Auto-synced" tab.

last_metadata_sync_at.description: RFC3339 of the last provider sync, or `null` if never synced.

BackfillOutcome.description:
Outcome of a phash backfill sweep — exposed via the admin
endpoint so the operator can see how many rows landed in each
category.

considered.description: Rows visited (had `phash IS NULL` at the start of the sweep).

errored.description: Rows that errored during DB update.

skipped.description: Rows skipped because the file was missing or undecodable.

BackupStorageView.description:
Rolled-up `.bak` backup-file footprint for a library (archive-rewrite M7).
Surfaced as a storage card on the library health page so operators can see
how much disk the edit/restore safety backups consume.

oldest_modified_at.description: RFC 3339; `None` when there are no backup files.

modified_at.description: RFC3339 modification time, or null if unavailable.

BatchAggregate.description: Live aggregate over a batch's child runs.

searched.description: Children whose search has finalized (completed / failed / awaiting_quota).

strong.description: `single_good` — one strong match, ready for "Accept all strong".

run_ids.description:
Restrict `all_needs_review` to these runs (the "Selected" subset).
Absent ⇒ every needs-review child in the batch ("All").

remainder.description: Targets beyond the per-request cap; re-trigger to apply the rest.

BatchChildRow.description: One child run in a batch, for the Review queue list.

label.description: Human label resolved from the run's stored query (series / issue name).

library_id.description: Parent library id — the review dialog's scope requires it.

outcome_kind.description: `MatchOutcomeKind` string once searched; `None` while in flight.

series_slug.description:
Parent series slug, for opening the review dialog (or deep-linking) to
the child's entity.

BatchCreatedResp.description:
Response for the batch-create endpoints. Mirrors [`RefreshOutcome`] plus the
new `batch_id` the caller deep-links the Review queue to.

items_total.description: Child runs created under this batch (the progress denominator).

jobs_coalesced.description:
Targets whose search coalesced onto an already-in-flight run (tracked
under that run, not this batch).

BatchRunTally.description:
Per-state tally of a batch's member runs — drives the dashboard progress
bar without the client refetching the run list.

exceeds_budget.description:
`true` when `items_total` exceeds the smallest provider daily budget —
the batch will span multiple windows (children park + auto-resume).

resume_eta.description:
Earliest `resume_after` across parked children (RFC3339), for the
"resumes ~HH:MM" hint.

BatchTotals.description:
Aggregated `ScanStats` counters summed across a batch's member runs — the
"what did this Scan all do overall" roll-up.

BulkAddMembersReq.description:
Body for `POST /me/collections/{id}/members/bulk-add`. Each
member follows the same `(entry_kind, ref_id)` shape as the
single-add endpoint. Multi-select toolbar (`<SelectionToolbar>`'s
"Add to collection…" action) builds this from the user's
`selectedIds` set.

Plan: `~/.claude/plans/multi-select-bulk-actions-1.0.md` (M3).

BulkAddMembersResp.description:
Response counts for the bulk-add endpoint. Lets the toast surface
"N added; M already in this collection; X skipped" without the
caller doing per-member round-trips.

added.description: New rows landed in the collection.

already_present.description:
Members that were already in the collection — the partial
unique fired, no row written. Idempotent from the user's
perspective.

invalid.description:
Members whose `entry_kind` / `ref_id` shape was malformed
(bad UUID, empty id, etc.). Same silent-skip semantics.

not_found.description:
Members whose ref_id didn't resolve to a real series or issue.
Silent skip; surfaced in the response counts so an
admin debugging "Add did nothing" can see the filter fired.

oneOf.description: Rotate the cover (page 0) by `degrees`.
Rotate every page by `degrees`.
Remove the first `count` pages (always leaves at least one page).
Remove the last `count` pages (always leaves at least one page).

BulkArchiveOp.description:
A single relative operation applied uniformly across a bulk selection
(M7). Unlike [`PageOp`], these don't carry absolute ordinals — they're
lowered to concrete `PageOp`s per issue once that issue's page count is
known (different archives have different page counts), so one bulk choice
like "remove the last 2 pages" does the right thing on every selected
issue.

issue_ids.description:
Issue ids to apply the op to. Issues that don't exist, live in a
non-writeback library, or aren't an editable format are skipped (and
reported), not failed.

op.description: The single relative op applied to every eligible issue.

queued.description: Number of per-issue edit jobs enqueued.

skipped.description: Issues that were skipped, with a reason each.

BulkMetadataPatch.description:
Per-field patch surface for the bulk-edit dialog
(`manga-and-bulk-metadata-1.0` M4). Every field is independently
optional. Sending `null` for a nullable field clears it; omitting
leaves it untouched.

**Credit fields are deliberately excluded** (writer, penciller,
cover_artist, editor, translator, inker, colorist, letterer):
these vary issue-to-issue in real series (guest artists, variant
covers, mid-series translator changes) and bulk-editing them
risks clobbering accurate per-issue credits. Continue using the
per-issue drawer for credits.

genre.description:
CSV. Replaces the field wholesale; for additive tag operations
the M5 dialog assembles the union client-side and sends it
here.

forbidden.description:
Issues the caller doesn't have library access to. Filtered
silently; surfaced in the response for admin debugging.

not_found.description: Issues whose id didn't resolve to an active row.

skipped.description:
Issues skipped because every targeted column was already set
AND `mode = "skip_if_set"`. (Counted once per issue, not per
field.)

BulkRemoveMembersReq.description:
Body for `POST /me/collections/{id}/members/bulk-remove`. Identifies
each member by its `(entry_kind, ref_id)` pair — the same shape the
bulk-add endpoint accepts — so the UI doesn't have to round-trip
to look up the `collection_entries.id` PK for each row.

Plan: `~/.claude/plans/multi-select-bulk-actions-1.0.md` (M4).

BulkRemoveMembersResp.description: Response counts for the bulk-remove endpoint.

invalid.description:
Malformed `(entry_kind, ref_id)` pairs. Same silent-skip
semantics as bulk-add.

not_present.description:
Members that weren't in the collection. Silent skip from the
user's perspective; surfaced here so the toast can say "5
removed (3 weren't in the collection anyway)" if it wants.

oneOf.description:
Match-outcome classification (matching-accuracy-1.0 M8).
`None` while the run is still searching; populated once it
completes. Drives the MetadataMatchDialog state — one-click
apply on `SingleGoodMatch`, warning copy on `SingleBadCover`,
flat list on `MultiGood` / `MultiBadCover`, empty state on
`NoMatches`.

CblDetailView.description:
Detail response — list metadata + aggregate counts only. Entries
moved to the paginated `/me/cbl-lists/{id}/entries` endpoint as of
2026-05-14; see [docs/dev plans](../../../../.claude/plans/list-pagination-completeness-1.0.md)
for rationale (large lists silently truncated at 500). The fields
on `CblListView.stats` carry the per-status counts that used to be
computed client-side from `entries`.

oneOf.description:
Hydrated matched issue. `None` for unmatched entries, and also
`None` when the matched issue lives in a library the caller
can't access (we still surface the entry — the position label
is meaningful even without a cover).

CblEntryHydratedView.description:
One entry + its hydrated `IssueSummaryView` (when `matched_issue_id`
resolves to an issue the caller can see). Returned by the paginated
entries endpoint so the consumption grid + Reading Order tab don't
need a second `/issues` round-trip.

next_cursor.description:
Opaque cursor for the next page. `None` when this page is the
last one for the given filter set.

total.description:
Count of all entries matching the filter. Returned ONLY on the
first page (cursor absent) — keeps subsequent fetches cheap.

stats.description:
Counts derived from `cbl_entries` for this list. Populated on
list + detail responses.

manual.description:
Subset of `matched`: entries the user resolved via the manual
match popover (not via the importer's automatic matcher).
**Already included in `matched`** — surfaced separately so the
Resolution tab can call out user overrides.

matched.description:
**Resolved entries** — every row whose `matched_issue_id IS NOT
NULL`, i.e. both auto-matched (`match_status='matched'`) AND
user-resolved overrides (`match_status='manual'`). Drives the
"Collection" pill, which is asking *"how many of this list's
books does the library have on disk?"* — the answer doesn't
care which path got the entry resolved.

The auto-vs-manual breakdown is still available below via
`manual`; subtract for the strict auto-only count.

read_count.description:
Count of matched entries whose issue the **calling user** has
finished. Drives the per-user reading-progress pill on the home
rail header. `0` for fresh accounts or when the caller has no
progress records yet.

CblWindowEntry.description:
One entry in the reading-window response. Mirrors `IssueSummaryView`
plus the per-user progress overlay so the rail can render finished /
in-progress / unread cards without a second round-trip.

last_page.description: Last page index the user reached on this issue (0 if unread).

position.description:
0-based position within the CBL — matches the `#N` badge other
surfaces use.

CblWindowPageView.description:
One page of a CBL reading window. `current_index` / `total_*`
fields are populated **only** on the initial page so subsequent
before/after fetches stay cheap and don't re-emit anchor-shaped
data the client already has.

current_index.description:
`initial` only: index of the user's current entry inside `items`
(after ACL filtering); `None` when every matched entry is
finished.

has_more_after.description:
True iff at least one matched entry exists with `position >
max_position`. Drives the rail's right-edge sentinel.

has_more_before.description:
True iff at least one matched entry exists with `position <
min_position`. Drives the rail's left-edge sentinel.

max_position.description:
Largest `position` value among the matched entries on this
page. Null when the page is empty. Used as the `after` cursor
for the next forward fetch.

min_position.description:
Smallest `position` value among the matched entries on this
page. Null when the page is empty. Used as the `before` cursor
for the next backward fetch.

total_entries.description: `initial` only: count of all entries (incl. unmatched).

total_matched.description: `initial` only: count of matched entries in the full list.

current_index.description:
Index within `items` of the user's current (first-unfinished)
matched entry. `None` when every matched entry is finished —
callers should show a "caught up" affordance.

total_entries.description:
Count of ALL entries (including unmatched). Surfaced for parity
with `useCblList().stats.total`.

total_matched.description:
Count of matched entries in the full list (so the rail can show
"Position N / total" without a second fetch).

total.description:
First-page total (entries belong to one collection, so this is a
cheap COUNT). Useful for "N items" headers without re-fetching.

oneOf.description: Populated when `entry_kind = 'issue'`.

Populated when `entry_kind = 'series'`. `None` when the
underlying series was cascade-deleted between the entry insert
and this read. Hydrated to the full `SeriesView` shape so the
home rail + collection detail page can reuse `<SeriesCard>`
without a synthesize-defaults dance.

CollectionIssueEntry.description:
One owned issue's identity + metadata-completeness status, for the
collection grid. `metadata_tier` is `"complete"` | `"partial"` |
`"needs_metadata"`; `missing_core` lists the absent core fields (empty when
complete).

special_type.description:
Spec §6.5 tag (`"Annual"`, `"OneShot"`, …) when classified; `None` for
ordinary main-run issues.

CollectionReportView.description:
Collection-completeness report for a series: how many issues are owned
versus expected, and **which** main-run issue numbers are missing.

The denominator (`total_expected`) comes from `series.total_issues`, which
the scanner resolves from a `series.json` sidecar or the max ComicInfo
`<Count>`. series.json carries only the *count*, never a per-issue
manifest — so interior `missing` numbers are *inferred* by interpolating
the integer run between the lowest and highest owned issue. `expected_source`
records this provenance; a future provider-backed exact manifest will flip
it to `"provider_manifest"` and make `missing` exact.

completeness_pct.description:
`total_owned / total_expected * 100`, `None` when `total_expected` is
`None`. Capped at 100 (over-collection still reads as complete).

completeness_state.description:
`"complete"` | `"incomplete"` | `"unknown"` — same vocabulary and CASE
semantics as the `collection_completeness` saved-view predicate.

expected_source.description:
`"series_total"` today (count-only). `"provider_manifest"` once an
exact provider issue list backs the report.

issues.description:
Every owned active issue with its metadata-completeness status, ordered
by `sort_number`. Lets the UI color each issue chip by status
(complete / partial / needs-metadata) and reveal the missing fields on
click — no separate per-issue request. Uses the same scorer as the
issue detail page.

specials.description:
Annuals, one-shots, TPBs, point issues (`#2.5`), and unnumbered files —
listed but excluded from the integer gap math.

total_expected.description:
Publisher-claimed total from `series.total_issues`. `None` when the
scanner couldn't resolve a count.

total_owned.description:
Active (non-removed) issues owned. Matches the saved-view
`collection_completeness` `active_count`, so this report and the
SeriesCard collection dot never disagree.

missing_core.description:
Core field keys that are absent ([`MetadataField::key`] or
[`EXTERNAL_ID_KEY`]). Empty for a [`CompletenessTier::Complete`] record.

missing_recommended.description: Recommended (non-gating) field keys that are absent.

score.description: Weighted fraction of core fields present, `0.0..=1.0`.

field_sources.description: Per-field candidate picks. A field absent here is not applied.

included.description:
The candidate `ordinal`s that contribute. Their `applied_at` is
flipped.

CompositeFieldRow.description: One field row across all included candidates.

chosen_ordinal.description:
Merge-policy default candidate `ordinal` for this field (`null`
when no included candidate has a value). The UI seeds its
per-field selection here.

decision.description:
`DiffDecision` for (current vs the chosen value) — reuses
`apply::classify_field` so the badge matches the single-candidate
pane exactly.

ordinal.description:
Candidate `ordinal` (unique within the run) whose value wins this
field.

CompositeProposal.description:
One candidate's proposed value for a field. Keyed by `ordinal`
(unique within a run) so multiple candidates from the same provider
are distinct columns.

CompositeProviderColumn.description: One provider column in the compare view's header.

cover_image_url.description: Cover URL for the "verify this is the same issue" thumbnail.

title.description: Series name / "name #number" for the column subtitle.

Condition.description:
One condition row in a filter DSL. `group_id` always 0 in v1; reserved
for nested-group support without a wire-format break.

value.description:
Shape varies by `(field, op)`. The compiler validates and rejects
mismatches with a clear error. See [`super::registry`] for the
matrix of allowed combinations.

label.description:
Free-form label so the user can tell their tokens apart.
1-80 characters; trimmed.

scope.description:
Optional scope tag — `read` (default) or `read+progress`.
Tokens with `read` can browse, page-stream, and download; the
progress-write surface (PUT `/opds/v1/issues/{id}/progress` and
the KOReader sync shim) requires `read+progress`.

oneOf.description: Direct URL to a `.cbl` file. Fetched server-side.
Pick from a configured `catalog_sources` row.

generate_page_thumbs_on_scan.description:
Set the per-library `generate_page_thumbs_on_scan` flag at
creation time. When true, the post-scan pipeline (including the
initial scan triggered by `scan_now`) enqueues page-strip
thumbnails alongside the always-on cover thumbnails. Defaults
to false; user can flip it later from library settings.

scan_now.description:
Explicitly enqueue the initial scan after creating the row.
Defaults to false so library creation is side-effect-light.

is_favorite.description: Star flag. Omit / false for a regular marker.

region.description:
`{ x, y, w, h, shape }` — rect dims as 0–100 percent floats
normalized to the page's natural pixel dims. Omit for
whole-page markers.

tags.description: Freeform tag list. Trimmed + de-duped + lowercased server-side.

kind.description:
`'filter_series'` or `'cbl'`. Validated server-side; mismatched
kind/body shape returns 422.

credit_count.description:
Total visible credit count across both junction tables (each
distinct (series_id) or (issue_id) counted once).

rails.description:
Series rails — one per role the creator held. Order matches
`roles`; each rail is sorted by series name. Empty when the
creator only has credits in libraries the caller can't see.

roles.description:
Distinct roles the creator holds across visible credits, ordered
canonically (writer → penciller → inker → colorist → letterer →
cover_artist → editor → translator → anything else alphabetical).

role.description:
Canonical role name as stored on the credit rows (e.g. `"writer"`,
`"penciller"`, `"cover_artist"`).

series.description:
Series the creator touched in this role, hydrated with the same
fields the library grid renders so the page can show full cards.

CrossLibHealthIssueView.description:
Cross-library health-issue row. Adds library context fields so the
admin findings table can render a Library column without N+1 lookups.

CrossLibScanRunView.description:
Cross-library scan-run row. Adds library context fields so the
admin findings table can render a Library column without N+1
lookups. Same shape as the per-library [`ScanRunView`] otherwise.

CursorPage_AdminUserStatsRow.description:
Cursor-paginated list response. `total` is populated only on the first
page of paginated lists where the count is cheap; bounded lists omit it.

CursorPage_CrossLibHealthIssueView.description:
Cursor-paginated list response. `total` is populated only on the first
page of paginated lists where the count is cheap; bounded lists omit it.

items.description:
Cross-library health-issue row. Adds library context fields so the
admin findings table can render a Library column without N+1 lookups.

CursorPage_CrossLibScanRunView.description:
Cursor-paginated list response. `total` is populated only on the first
page of paginated lists where the count is cheap; bounded lists omit it.

items.description:
Cross-library scan-run row. Adds library context fields so the
admin findings table can render a Library column without N+1
lookups. Same shape as the per-library [`ScanRunView`] otherwise.

CursorPage_LibraryEventView.description:
Cursor-paginated list response. `total` is populated only on the first
page of paginated lists where the count is cheap; bounded lists omit it.

library_name.description:
Joined `library.name` so the cross-library activity log renders without
an N+1. `null` if the library row was since deleted.

CursorPage_LogWidgetView.description:
Cursor-paginated list response. `total` is populated only on the first
page of paginated lists where the count is cheap; bounded lists omit it.

CursorPage_PageView.description:
Cursor-paginated list response. `total` is populated only on the first
page of paginated lists where the count is cheap; bounded lists omit it.

description.description:
Optional free-form description rendered under the title. `None`
(or absent) hides the descriptor row.

pin_count.description:
Count of `user_view_pin` rows where `pinned = true` for this
(user, page) pair. Lets the sidebar render a badge or the page
picker show "n / 12" without fetching every pin row.

show_in_sidebar.description:
Whether this page appears in the sidebar nav. Computed from
`user_sidebar_entries` — missing override means visible (the
default). System pages always show via the builtin Home entry
regardless of this flag.

CursorPage_ScanBatchView.description:
Cursor-paginated list response. `total` is populated only on the first
page of paginated lists where the count is cheap; bounded lists omit it.

library_count.description: Number of runs that adopted the batch (newly-enqueued libraries).

runs.description: Live per-state breakdown of the member runs.

CursorPage_String.description:
Cursor-paginated list response. `total` is populated only on the first
page of paginated lists where the count is cheap; bounded lists omit it.

applies_last_7_days.description:
Count of successful `metadata_apply` audit rows in the last
7 days.

providers.description:
Per-provider quota snapshots — only populated when the
provider is configured + enabled.

series_matched.description:
Series with at least one row in `external_ids` from a
configured provider source.

series_total.description:
Total series rows the operator has — denominator for the
"matched / unmatched" headline.

series_unmatched.description:
`series_total - series_matched` (precomputed so the UI can
render directly).

DeadLetterView.description:
Per-queue counts of jobs apalis has given up on (moved to `{queue}:dead`
after exhausting attempts). `total > 0` is the operator's cue that work is
failing permanently and silently; the per-queue breakdown points at which.

DeepValidateResp.description:
Response for `POST /libraries/{slug}/validate-deeply`. Returns
immediately with `202 Accepted`; the deep-validate runs in a
`tokio::spawn` task and emits per-page health-issues as it goes.

DeleteLibraryResp.description:
Response for `DELETE /libraries/{id}` — small summary for the audit log
and any UI that wants to confirm what got purged.

deleted_issues.description: Issue rows removed (cascades from libraries → series → issues).

thumbs_swept.description:
On-disk thumbnail directories swept. Best-effort — disk errors are
logged but don't block the SQL delete.

changes_count.description:
Count of rows whose decision would actually write a value
(`would_fill` + `would_replace`). Lets the preview header
summarize "5 changes" without the client re-walking the rows.

external_id_conflicts.description:
Only present when the candidate carries external_ids that
disagree with user-set rows on the same entity.

external_ids_new.description:
Provider IDs the candidate brought that the entity doesn't
have yet (no conflict — would just add).

rows.description:
Per-field rows, ordered by enum declaration (groups
scalars-then-junctions-then-covers).

scope.description:
`"series" | "issue"` — echoes the run scope so the client
can render the right header copy.

source.description:
`"comicvine" | "metron"` — which provider supplied the
candidate. Drives the "Apply from <provider>" copy.

dow.description:
0 = Sunday, 1 = Monday, …, 6 = Saturday. Matches Postgres
`EXTRACT(DOW FROM …)` so the client doesn't need to remap.

dry_run.description:
When true, validate the ops and return the would-be page count
without touching the archive.

oneOf.description: `dry_run=true` — validation succeeded; here's what would happen.
Edit job enqueued; the UI waits for the scan-completed WebSocket.

configured.description:
`true` when the live sender is a real transport (Lettre or Mock),
`false` when SMTP is unset and the Noop fallback is installed.

dau.description: Distinct users active in the trailing 1 day window ending on `date`.

mau.description: Distinct users active in the trailing 30-day window ending on `date`.

wau.description: Distinct users active in the trailing 7-day window ending on `date`.

devices_30d.description:
Aggregate device breakdown across all (non-excluded) users over the
last 30 days.

series.description: Trailing-window DAU/WAU/MAU samples per day, last 90 days.

first_read_at.description:
Best-effort first-read timestamp (earliest reading_session
`started_at` for this user+issue). `None` when no session
rows survived for the issue.

is_reread.description: Heuristic: `total_sessions > 1` is a reread. Refined in M6+.

total_sessions.description: Count of finalized reading_sessions for this user+issue.

span_days.description:
`(finished_at - started_at)` whole days. `None` when
`started_at` is missing.

started_at.description:
Earliest `finished_at` across the series's matched issues —
when the user first finished any issue in the series.

total_active_ms.description: Sum of all `active_ms` across the series's sessions.

total_issues.description:
Active issues in the series. Always > 0 (you can't finish
a series with zero issues).

body_preview.description:
First ~80 chars of the marker body. `None` when the marker
has no body (bookmarks / favorites typically don't).

ExternalIdConflictRow.description:
External-ID conflict row — surfaces in the preview pane as an
amber row with a Keep mine / Use theirs control.

proposed_external_id.description:
The candidate's proposed external id (always different from
`current_external_id`; same-value rows are filtered out).

ExternalIdNewRow.description:
Provider external id the entity doesn't carry yet — apply will
just add the row, no conflict.

Field.description:
All filterable fields. Per-field metadata (kind, allowed ops, SQL
column) lives in [`super::registry`]. Adding a field is a two-step:
add a variant here + a registry entry. The OpenAPI schema mirrors this
enum so the M5 field-picker can iterate variants without a separate
JSON constant.

FieldProvenanceRow.description:
One field's provenance: which source set it, when, and (for provider
sources) which external record it came from.

FlushMetadataDriftResp.description: Response body for the metadata-drift flush endpoint.

enqueued_rewrites.description: Number of per-issue rewrite jobs enqueued.

skipped.description:
Number of issues that were drift-eligible but couldn't be
composed (missing archive, busy mutex, …). Operator can re-run
the flush to retry.

build_epoch.description:
Build timestamp (Unix epoch seconds, UTC). Lets `just dev-status`
flag servers that are still running an old binary after a rebuild.

build_sha.description:
Short git SHA captured at build time. Suffixed with `-dirty` when the
working tree had uncommitted changes during compilation. `unknown`
when not built inside a git checkout (e.g. tarball builds).

HideEventReq.description:
Body for `POST /me/reading-log/hide` and `/unhide`. Identifies a
single event in the feed by its `kind` and the raw source id
(without the `iss-fin:` / `ses:` / `mrk:` prefix the wire payload
uses on `ReadingLogEventView.id` — clients strip that before
calling).

kind.description:
One of `issue_finished | session_completed | marker_created`.
`series_finished` is rejected (derived event with no source
row to flag).

source_id.description:
For `issue_finished`: the `issues.id` BLAKE3 hex.
For `session_completed`: the `reading_sessions.id` UUID.
For `marker_created`: the `markers.id` UUID.

image_url.description: URL the frontend should render `` from. Points at the local byte endpoint (`/issues/{id}/covers/{cover_id}`) when the cover was downloaded to disk, else falls back to `source_url` (provider CDN hotlink). `None` only when neither exists.

source_url.description:
CDN URL the provider returned. Kept for attribution + the
"open original" link; not the primary render source once the
image is stored locally.

fallback_primary_url.description:
Fallback URL the frontend can render when no `issue_cover` row
exists (legacy issues whose primary cover is the page-thumb
pipeline). Always points at page 0's cover thumb.

additional_links.description:
User-curated extra links beyond `web_url` (which mirrors ComicInfo).
Each entry has a required `url` and optional `label`.

allow_archive_writeback.description:
Whether the parent library has archive writeback enabled. Gates
the admin "Edit archive…" affordance in the UI
(`archive-rewrite-1.0` M3). Populated by the issue-detail handler;
defaults false (not stored on the issue row).

comicvine_id.description:
External-database IDs. ComicVine encodes the ID as `4000-N` for issues;
the parser strips the prefix so callers see the bare integer.

cover_page_index.description:
0-based index of the page used as the cover. Surfaced so the page
editor can highlight the current cover. `archive-rewrite-1.0`.

creator_slugs.description:
Creator-name → slug map covering every credit name listed in
the per-role CSV fields above (writer/penciller/inker/…). Built
from `issue_credits.person_id` joined to `person`; missing
entries fall back to the legacy library-grid filter in the UI.
Empty until the get_one handler populates it.

file_size.description:
File size in bytes from the disk row at the last scan. Surfaced in the
admin "Details" tab alongside `file_path`.

last_metadata_sync_at.description:
ISO-8601 timestamp of the last provider metadata sync for this issue
(`issue.last_metadata_sync_at`). `None` when never synced. Surfaced in
the Metadata tab's freshness section.

last_rewrite_at.description:
ISO-8601 timestamp of the last in-place rewrite of this issue's
archive bytes (from `metadata-sidecar-writeback-1.0` M3+ or
`archive-rewrite-1.0` M2+). `None` when Folio has never
rewritten the file. Surfaces in the UI as a "Metadata last
written {kind} on {date}" badge.

last_rewrite_kind.description:
`"sidecar"` (XML refresh) or `"edit"` (page bytes touched).
Paired with [`Self::last_rewrite_at`].

library_cbr_convert_confirmed.description:
Whether the parent library has acknowledged the CBR→CBZ conversion
at least once (`library.cbr_convert_confirmed_at` is set). The page
editor uses this to decide whether to show the one-time conversion
explainer when editing a `.cbr`. `archive-rewrite-1.0` M6.

library_default_reading_direction.description:
Parent library's `default_reading_direction` (`"ltr"` | `"rtl"`).
The reader consults this as a fallback below ComicInfo
`<Manga>` and the user's per-account preference but above the
hard-coded LTR default. Plumbed by the issue-detail handler;
not stored on the issue row itself.

oneOf.description:
Metadata-completeness assessment ("does this issue need metadata
pulled?"). Populated by the issue-detail handler; `None` on list
endpoints (the cheaper `IssueSummaryView.metadata_completeness_tier`
carries the at-a-glance signal there).

pages.description:
Per-page metadata, deserialized from the issue's stored JSON. Empty when
the parse failed or the source had no `<Pages>` block.
`value_type` keeps the parsers crate framework-free; the actual schema
fields (image, type, double_page, image_width, image_height, …) are
stable and documented in `crates/parsers/src/comicinfo.rs::PageInfo`.

series_reading_direction.description:
Parent series' `reading_direction` override. Consulted by the
reader above the user and library defaults but below ComicInfo
`<Manga>`. `None` = "Auto" (series has no override). Plumbed by
the issue-detail handler; not stored on the issue row itself.
See `manga-and-bulk-metadata-1.0` M2.

series_slug.description: Slug of the parent series — handy for nested URL construction.

slug.description: URL-safe identifier, unique within the parent series.

user_edited.description:
Names of fields the user has overridden via `PATCH /issues/{id}`. The
scanner skips these on a rescan. Surfaced so the UI can flag rows as
"edited" and offer a "revert to ComicInfo" affordance later.

user_rating.description: Calling user's 0..=5 rating for this issue. `None` when unset.

label.description: Display label. `null` falls back to the URL host on the client.

items.description:
Every marker the calling user has on this issue, across all
pages. The reader overlay slices client-side by `page_index`.

snippet.description:
Search-result excerpt with `<mark>…</mark>` tags around matched
terms — same shape as `SeriesView.snippet`. Omitted when the
issue's free-text fields (summary / story_arc / characters)
contain no highlightable fragment for the query.

series_name.description:
Parent series name, denormalized so card components can fall back
to `"<series> #<number>"` when the issue has no title. Populated
by endpoints that have the series row in scope (rails, CBL
window, collections, search); `None` on endpoints where adding
the join would cost more than the user-visible benefit.

special_type.description:
Spec §6.5 classification: `"Special"`, `"Annual"`, `"OneShot"`,
`"TPB"`, or `None` for ordinary numbered issues. Drives the
reader's Specials & Extras section on the series detail page.

html_url.description: Browse URL on the host's release page.

library_ids.description:
Final set of library ids the user should be granted access to. The
server replaces the user's `library_user_access` rows with this list.

library_name.description:
Joined `library.name` so the cross-library activity log renders without
an N+1. `null` if the library row was since deleted.

allow_archive_writeback.description:
Hard prerequisite for any code path that mutates archive bytes
(sidecar writeback + page edits). Default false; admin flips on
when ready to start rewriting files for this library.

archive_backup_retain_count.description:
How many `.bak` siblings to keep per archive after a rewrite.
1..=5; default 1.

archive_backup_retain_days.description:
Auto-prune `.bak` files older than this many days. Default 30;
`0` = keep forever.

archive_writeback_jpeg_quality.description:
Encoder quality (60..=100) the page editor uses when re-encoding a
rotated / replaced JPEG page. Default 92. PNG / WebP pages are
rewritten losslessly and ignore this. `archive-rewrite-1.0`.

auto_convert_cbr_on_scan.description:
When true AND `allow_archive_writeback` is also true, the scanner
converts each `.cbr` it finds into a sibling `.cbz` in place (keeping
the original as `.cbr.bak`) and ingests the `.cbz`. Default false.

cbr_convert_confirmed_at.description:
RFC3339 timestamp of the operator's first acknowledgement of the
CBR→CBZ conversion for this library, or `null` if they haven't
acknowledged yet. The page editor uses this to decide whether to
show the conversion confirm dialog. `archive-rewrite-1.0` M6.

filename_assume_issue_one.description:
When true the scanner's filename inferer assumes issue `1`
when no issue number is detected. Matching-accuracy-1.0 M7.

filename_ignore_leading_numbers.description:
When true the scanner's filename inferer drops a leading
numeric token before parsing the series. Matching-accuracy-1.0 M7.

generate_page_thumbs_on_scan.description:
When true, the post-scan pipeline auto-enqueues page-strip thumbnails
alongside the always-on cover thumbnails. See migration
`m20261211_000001_generate_page_thumbs_on_scan` for rationale.

metadata_auto_apply_strong_matches.description:
When true, non-manual searches that produce a strict
SingleGood outcome auto-apply the top candidate. User-edit
precedence still fires. Matching-accuracy-1.0 M12.

metadata_publisher_blacklist.description:
Publisher names the matcher's pre-filter should drop before
scoring. Comparison is case-insensitive against the
title-sanitized form so "DC Comics" / "dc comics" / "DC" all
match the same entry. Matching-accuracy-1.0 M3.

metadata_writeback_enabled.description:
When true AND `allow_archive_writeback` is also true, provider
apply takes the XML-first path: worker writes ComicInfo.xml +
MetronInfo.xml into the archive and enqueues a scoped rescan.
Per-library so operators migrate gradually.

root_path_writable.description:
Whether the library's `root_path` is on a writable mount. When
false the admin UI disables the archive-writeback toggle and the
PATCH handler refuses to enable it — rewrites can't land on a
read-only mount. Computed per-request via `statvfs`.

scan_schedule_cron.description: Cron expression governing the scheduled scan, or `null` if disabled.

path.description:
Canonical absolute path of the listed directory. The picker uses
this for the breadcrumb and as the value to drill back up from.

root.description:
Canonical absolute path of the configured library root. The
picker uses this to know when "up" should be disabled.

registration_open.description:
Self-serve registration is open. False locks the system to the
existing user set + the first-user admin bootstrap.

smtp_configured.description:
SMTP wired (host non-empty). Without it, email verification + reset
flows fall back to bypass-on-register.

domain.description: `server` | `library` — which observability stream this entry belongs to.

error_code.description:
Convenience: `fields["error_code"]` lifted out for the Server-log
error-code facet. `null` for non-error events.

next.description:
Progressive-enhancement redirect target for the no-JS form path.
Validated through `is_safe_redirect_target` before any 303. Ignored
by the JSON path.

watermark.description:
Highest id in the response (or `since`, if empty). Pass back as
`?since=` to tail.

MainRunReport.description: The integer-numbered backbone of a series and its inferred gaps.

min.description:
Lowest / highest owned main-run number (as f64), `None` when the run is
empty.

trailing_missing.description:
Count expected beyond `max` when `total_expected > max` (e.g. own up to
#4 with `total_expected = 6` → `2`).

MarkerCountView.description:
Cheap `SELECT COUNT(*)` over the caller's markers. Drives the
sidebar badge — TanStack caches it for 60s so the navigation
doesn't fan out a full /me/markers fetch.

series_name.description:
Hydrated series + issue context so a marker hit knows enough
to render a "Jump to page" link + a series label without a
second round-trip.

snippet.description:
`ts_headline()` excerpt over `body` and `selection->>'text'`
with `<mark>…</mark>` around matched terms. Same sanitiser as
the series + issue snippets on the client.

MarkerSearchView.description:
Global-search M2 — markers as a 4th category. The hit shape is
trimmed of fields the modal / `/search` page don't need
(color, tags, full timestamps) and carries the `<mark>`-wrapped
snippet inline. ACL is per-caller (`/me/...`), so a hit lists
only markers the user owns.

items.description:
Distinct tag set across the caller's markers, with a usage
count each. Sorted by count desc, then alpha.

body.description: Markdown body. Required when `kind = 'note'`, optional elsewhere.

is_favorite.description:
Star flag. Any marker can be favorited; the /bookmarks
"Favorites" chip filters on this rather than on kind.

kind.description:
`'bookmark' | 'note' | 'highlight'`. Favorite is no longer a
kind — see `is_favorite`.

region.description:
`{ x, y, w, h, shape }` — see the migration doc for the precise
shape contract. `None` when the marker is page-level.

selection.description:
`{ text?, image_hash?, ocr_confidence? }` — populated by the
reader's text / image-aware highlight modes.

series_name.description:
Hydrated for the global `/me/markers` feed so the index page can
render series + issue context without a second round-trip. Omitted
from `POST`/`PATCH` responses and the per-issue lookup (those
callers already have the surrounding context).

tags.description: Per-user freeform tags. Empty when unset.

MatchOutcomeView.description:
Discriminated view of the matcher's outcome classification.
Vocabulary mirrors [`crate::metadata::match_outcome::MatchOutcomeKind`]:
`single_good`, `multi_good`, `single_bad_cover`, `multi_bad_cover`,
`no_match`.

`top_hamming` is the top candidate's cover-pHash Hamming distance
when a phash pair was available, else `null`. `matched_via_alternate`
tells the UI whether the top match came from a variant (drives the
"via alternate cover" badge).

last_7d.description:
Distribution of outcomes within the trailing 7 days. The
shape mirrors what the M8 dialog will use, so the dashboard
can speak the same vocabulary once cover-decides ships in M4.

total_7d.description: Total rows in each window — denominator for any percentage UI.

oneOf.description: M4: accent palette token.

activity_tracking_enabled.description: M6a: per-user opt-out for reading-activity capture.

default_cover_solo.description:
Default for the reader's "cover stands alone in double-page view"
toggle. Per-series localStorage overrides at runtime.

oneOf.description: M4: reader default fit mode.

v0.3.44 / v0.3.45: reader page-turn animation. `null` means
"use the reader's built-in default" (currently `slide`); fresh
users start here. Webtoon view ignores this regardless.

default_page_strip.description: M4: when true the reader opens with the page strip visible.

oneOf.description:
Phase 3: per-user reader direction preference. `null` means "auto"
(the reader still falls back to ComicInfo `Manga=YesAndRightToLeft`
detection per series).
M4: reader default view mode.
M4: density token.

email_editable.description: True when `/me/account` accepts email edits for this user.

exclude_from_aggregates.description:
Stats v2: opt-out from server-wide aggregates. When true, admin
dashboards exclude this user's sessions from totals/top-series. Does
not affect personal `/me/reading-stats`.

keybinds.description:
M4: per-action keybind overrides for the reader. Empty object means
"use defaults".

language.description:
Human-URLs M3: BCP-47 language tag used for the `NEXT_LOCALE` cookie
and next-intl message bundle selection.

max_rails_per_page.description:
Per-user override of the home-page rail cap. Replaces the
previous hard-coded `MAX_PIN_COUNT = 12` constant. Range
1..=50 enforced by DB CHECK + PATCH validation. Default 12.

password_editable.description: True when `/me/account` accepts local password changes for this user.

reading_idle_ms.description: M6a: idle threshold (ms) after which the client ends the session.

reading_min_active_ms.description: M6a: minimum accumulated active ms before a session is recorded.

reading_min_pages.description: M6a: minimum distinct pages before a session is recorded.

show_marker_count.description:
Markers M8: when true, /settings renders a count badge on the
Bookmarks sidebar row. Default false.

oneOf.description: M4: theme token.

timezone.description: M6a: IANA timezone string for daily-bucket aggregations.

MetadataCompletenessSummary.description:
Issue-level completeness rollup for a series. `complete` counts active
issues meeting the issue core criteria; `total` is the active-issue count.

MetadataOverviewView.description:
Total metadata overview for an issue: completeness, source files,
freshness, per-field provenance, external IDs, and user-pinned fields.

cbl_list_id.description:
Set when `source == "cbl"`. The id of the CBL list (NOT the
saved-view id the caller sent) — useful for downstream UI that
wants to deep-link into the CBL detail.

cbl_list_name.description:
Set when `source == "cbl"`. The CBL list's human-readable name,
for the chrome pill's "in {list}" sub-label.

cbl_param_was_stale.description:
`true` when the caller passed `?cbl=<id>` but the current issue
wasn't in that CBL (entry deleted, stale share-link, etc.). The
web layer uses this as a signal to strip the dead param from the
URL via `router.replace` so a refresh / shared link no longer
carries the stale reference.

cbl_position.description:
Set when `source == "cbl"`. 1-based position of the *target*
entry within the CBL (so the reader can show "Issue 4 of 24").

cbl_total.description: Set when `source == "cbl"`. Total matched entries in the CBL.

oneOf.description:
Top On Deck card to show as a peek when `source == "none"`. M1
always returns `None`; populating it remains deferred work since
the on_deck composition isn't factored as a reusable helper yet.
The web layer falls back to a "Browse the library" CTA when this
field is absent.

The next-up issue, denormalized with its parent series slug + name
so the reader can render the pill / end-card without a follow-up
fetch. `None` iff `source == "none"`.

bytes_on_disk.description:
Recursive sum of bytes under `cache_dir`. `0` when
`present = false`.

cache_dir.description:
Absolute path the loader actually reads from. May not exist
yet — pair with `present`.

expected_bytes_approx.description:
Typical post-download size — informational, used by the UI to
render a download-progress bar before the model is fully cached.

present.description:
`true` if at least one model file is on disk under
`cache_dir`. For HF entries this is "the snapshot folder
holds files"; for tessdata, "`eng.traineddata` exists".

source.description:
Where the binary fetches this model from on first init.
`"huggingface.co/<repo>"` or `"tessdata_best (build script)"`.

hf_home.description:
Resolved `HF_HOME` — useful for operators wanting to
pre-stage models on disk in air-gapped deploys.

tessdata_dir.description:
Resolved tessdata directory; see module doc for resolution
order.

detect.description:
When `true`, run `comic-text-detector` over the page to snap
the user's drag rect to the tightest enclosing speech-bubble
polygon. When `false` (or omitted — that's the default as of
v0.3.26), the recognizer runs on the user's rect verbatim.

Why default off: on CPU-constrained hosts the detector costs
~50 s on the *first* bubble of a page (subsequent bubbles on
the same page hit the polygon cache and run ~200 ms). That
cold-start makes the snap-to-bubble feature unusable in
practice. Operators on fast hardware can flip the request
flag to `true` to opt in; a future server config knob will
let them flip the default. The recognizer-only path
(~200 ms total) matches what the old browser-side
tesseract.js delivered, just better quality (real Tesseract
5 + tessdata_best, not the in-browser fast variant).

lang.description:
`"western"` or `"manga"`. Omitted → the server resolves a
default: `series.text_language` if set, else `manga` when the
series reads right-to-left, else `western`. The response
echoes the resolved value in `lang`.

page.description: 0-based page index, matching the reader's URL convention.

region.description:
User's drag rectangle in page-pixel coordinates. All four
fields are unsigned pixels; w/h must be > 0 and the rect
must fit inside the decoded page bounds.

confidence.description:
Mean confidence in `[0.0, 1.0]` over the words kept by the
cleanup pass (Tesseract); manga-ocr reports `1.0` (or `0.0`
for an empty result) since the greedy decoder doesn't expose
per-token probabilities.

lang.description:
Recognizer the pipeline actually ran (`"western"` |
`"manga"`) — the request override or the server-side
resolution chain. `default` so result-cache payloads written
before this field existed still deserialize.

raw_text.description:
Engine output before cleanup. Present only when cleanup
changed the text — a debugging aid, and the raw material for
golden-fixture authoring when users report junk output.

oneOf.description:
Detector's snap-to-bubble rectangle in page-pixel coords.
`None` when no detector hit overlapped the user's region —
the recognizer ran on the user's rect verbatim.

text.description:
Recognized text after the postprocess cleanup pass
([`crate::ocr::postprocess`]).

words.description:
Per-word boxes in page-pixel coords, post-cleanup. `None`
when the engine doesn't expose word data (manga-ocr) or the
iterator yielded nothing.

OcrWord.description: One recognized word: cleaned text + confidence + page-pixel box.

link_local_by_verified_email.description:
True when a first OIDC login with a verified email may auto-link onto
an existing local account (instead of returning `auth.email_in_use`).
Surfaced because it trusts the IdP's `email_verified` for linking.

trust_unverified_email.description:
True when `COMIC_OIDC_TRUST_UNVERIFIED_EMAIL` is set. Surfaced because
it materially weakens email-claim trust.

issuer.description:
The OIDC issuer URL the admin is about to save. Used as the base
for `${issuer}/.well-known/openid-configuration`.

scopes_supported.description:
Truncated to the most useful fields above. The full doc is large
and not all of it is admin-interesting; if a future need arises
the raw JSON can be added under a separate field.

cbl_saved_view_id.description:
Saved-view id (kind=`cbl`) wrapping this CBL list. Web
threads it onto the reader URL as `?cbl=<id>` so the next-up
resolver keeps picking from the list across page turns.
Wrapper-less lists are no longer candidates (ghost guard),
so cards always carry `Some` in practice; the field stays
`Option` for wire-compat.

Tiebreak when multiple saved views match: user-owned wins
over system-owned (NULL `user_id`); within a tier, lowest
`id` wins. Stable + cheap; the picker UI can pick a
different one later if needed.

position.description:
1-based position of the entry within its CBL list (matches the
"#N" badge the CBL detail UI shows).

OnDeckCard.description:
Discriminated union for the On Deck rail. Each card represents one
"what's next" suggestion — either the next-unread issue in a series the
user has finished at least one issue of, or the next-unread entry in a
CBL list the user is working through.

active_readers_now.description: Distinct users with a heartbeat in the last 5 minutes.

reads_per_day.description:
Daily reads-volume buckets for the last 14 days (UTC). Each row
aggregates across all users — never per-user, so the dashboard never
leaks anyone's reading habits.

sessions_today.description: Reading sessions started in the last 24h.

top_series_all_users.description:
System-wide most-read series in the last 30 days. Aggregated across
users; consumed by `/admin/stats`. Capped at 10 entries.

sec_per_page.description:
`active_ms / (1000 * distinct_pages_read)` — average seconds per
distinct page within that session.

started_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of the session start.

page_count.description:
The archive's *actual* page count, read live from the file. Authoritative
over the DB's `issue.page_count`, which can drift (stale scan, or sourced
from a ComicInfo `<PageCount>`); the editor builds its tiles from this so
it never shows a phantom page that isn't in the archive.

oneOf.description: Drop the page at `ordinal`.

Replace the whole page list order with `new_order` — a permutation
of `0..current_len`.
Rotate the page at `ordinal` by `degrees` (clockwise).
Replace the page at `ordinal` with a previously-staged upload.

Apply an image-adjustment chain (brightness/contrast, levels,
sharpen, despeckle, crop) to the page at `ordinal`.

PageOp.description:
One page-level operation. Ordinals are 0-based positions in the page
list *as it stands when the op is applied* (ops are sequential).

description.description:
Optional free-form description rendered under the title. `None`
(or absent) hides the descriptor row.

pin_count.description:
Count of `user_view_pin` rows where `pinned = true` for this
(user, page) pair. Lets the sidebar render a badge or the page
picker show "n / 12" without fetching every pin row.

show_in_sidebar.description:
Whether this page appears in the sidebar nav. Computed from
`user_sidebar_entries` — missing override means visible (the
default). System pages always show via the builtin Home entry
regardless of this flag.

config.description:
Replacement config blob. Replaces the entire `config` column
— clients merge with the previous value client-side. The
PATCH name is preserved because the row's other fields stay
the same and config is what the form actually edits.

slug.description:
Stable URL slug for `/creators/<slug>`. Populated from the
`person` table (M8 of the search-improvements plan). `None`
when the name hasn't been backfilled yet — caller falls back
to the legacy `?library=all&credits=<name>` URL.

PinReq.description:
Body for `POST /me/saved-views/{id}/pin`.

Multi-page rails M3: the same view can now be pinned to multiple
pages in a single call. Omitting the body (or sending an empty
`page_ids`) targets the caller's system Home page so existing
web clients keep working until they migrate to the multi-page
picker (M5/M6).

page_id.description:
Page this row belongs to. Mirrors the request's `page_id` (or the
resolved system page when the legacy no-body form was used).

PreferencesReq.description:
`PATCH /me/preferences` request body. Every field is optional; when a key
is absent the prior value is preserved. To clear a stored value, send
`null` (where the type allows).

activity_tracking_enabled.description: M6a: opt-out toggle. `false` disables the client tracker hook.

default_cover_solo.description: Default cover-solo toggle; absent leaves the prior value untouched.

oneOf.description:
`null` clears the preference (server falls back to the reader's
built-in default of `slide`).
`null` clears the preference.

exclude_from_aggregates.description:
Stats v2: privacy toggle. When true, admin server-wide aggregates
exclude this user's sessions.

keybinds.description: Replace the entire keybinds object. Send `{}` to clear all overrides.

language.description:
Human-URLs M3: BCP-47 language tag. Validated against the supported
locale list — sending an unknown value 400s rather than silently
falling back.

max_rails_per_page.description:
Override the home-page rail cap. 1..=50; validated server-
side and at the DB CHECK constraint. Default 12 preserves
the prior behaviour for users who never touch this.

show_marker_count.description:
Markers M8: per-user toggle for the Bookmarks sidebar count
badge. Default false.

timezone.description:
M6a: IANA timezone string. Server validates it's parseable; an unknown
zone is rejected so the heatmap can't silently fall back to UTC.

oneOf.description:
The issue immediately preceding the current one in `sort_number`
order, or `null` when the current issue is the first in the series.

updated_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of the most recent progress write.

ProviderBudget.description: Per-provider remaining budget for the batch's pacing warning.

configured.description:
`true` when the credential is set but the master toggle is off
— UI surfaces a "Enable to test" hint in that state.

enabled.description:
`true` when an API key / credentials are set AND the master
`metadata.<provider>.enabled` toggle is on.

PublicAuthConfigView.description:
Minimum surface for the unauthenticated `/sign-in` page. Mirrors a
subset of [`AuthConfigView`] but returns only the booleans the UI
needs to render the right CTAs — issuer/client_id are intentionally
withheld.

oidc_enabled.description:
True when the OIDC provider is wired AND `auth_mode` permits it.
When false, the sign-in page hides the SSO button.

password_recovery_enabled.description:
True when SMTP is configured (host non-empty). When false, the
`/auth/forgot-password` UI renders a "contact your administrator"
banner instead of the email form — a forgot-password submission
would otherwise silently fail when the operator hasn't filled in
SMTP credentials. Anonymous-safe; just reflects whether
`smtp.host` is set.

registration_open.description:
True when local registration is open. False locks the system to
the existing user set + first-user admin bootstrap.

running_jobs_may_finish.description:
Redis queue clearing is immediate, but a job already executing in a
worker may finish and emit its normal completion events.

archive_edit.description: Pending archive page-edit jobs (single + bulk; M7).

total.description: Sum across all queues — convenient for the topbar pill.

RatingView.description:
Response shape — mirrors what the GET endpoints inline so callers don't
need to refetch to refresh local state.

id.description:
Synthetic stable id: `{kind-prefix}:{underlying-id}`. Same id
across pages — clients dedupe with it.

is_hidden.description:
`true` when this event was hidden via
`POST /me/reading-log/hide` and is only being surfaced
because the caller passed `?include_hidden=true`. UI uses it
to render a faded card + an "Unhide" affordance. Default
`false` is `skip_serializing_if`-elided so the wire payload
stays the same shape for clients that don't use the feature.

oneOf.description:
Hydrated issue row (when the event has one). `series_finished`
is the only kind with `issue = None`; the others always
resolve.

occurred_at.description: RFC3339 — the canonical sort key for the feed.

payload.description:
Kind-specific metadata. Different schema per kind, see
`EventPayload` variants.

oneOf.description:
Hydrated series row (when the event has one). All four kinds
have a series; `None` only on the very rare case where the
referenced series was deleted between event capture and the
hydrate query.

next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor; pass back as `?cursor=` to fetch the next page.

issue_title.description:
Joined from `issues.title`. The list endpoint populates this; the
upsert response leaves it null since the client doesn't render
upsert results inline.

completion.description:
Stats v2 — distinct issues the user has read past the last page (or
flagged finished in `progress_records`). Used with
`totals.distinct_issues` to compute completion rate.

dow_hour.description:
Stats v2 — sparse 7×24 day-of-week × hour grid (in user's timezone).
Only cells with sessions are emitted.

first_read_at.description: Stats v2 — first session's `started_at` within the scope.

last_read_at.description: Stats v2 — last session's `started_at` within the scope.

pace_series.description:
Stats v2 — per-session pace samples: `(started_at, sec_per_page)`.
Sessions with `distinct_pages_read < 3` are excluded to drop noise.

reread_top_issues.description:
Stats v2 — top issues by read count (number of sessions). For
"most rereads" displays.

reread_top_series.description: Stats v2 — top series by total session count.

time_of_day.description:
Stats v2 — 4 time-of-day buckets (morning/afternoon/evening/night)
derived from `dow_hour`.

top_creators.description:
Stats v2 — top creators across read series, partitioned per role
(writer/penciller/inker/colorist/letterer/cover_artist). Up to 10 per
role. Source: `series_credits`.

top_genres.description: M6b — most-read genres derived from issue `genre` CSVs.

top_publishers.description: M6b — most-read publishers (series.publisher with issue.publisher fallback).

top_series.description:
M6b — most-read series in the range. Capped at 10. Empty when the
query is already issue-scoped (a single series is implied).

top_tags.description: M6b — most-read tags derived from issue `tags` CSVs.

next.description:
Progressive-enhancement redirect target for the no-JS form path.
Validated through `is_safe_redirect_target` before any 303. Ignored
by the JSON path (the client routes via `useRouter().push`).

series_slug.description:
URL slug of the parent series — paired with `slug` it forms the
`/series/{series_slug}/issues/{slug}/(restore|confirm-removal)` path.

ids.description:
Full set of widget ids in the new order. Server rejects when
the set doesn't match the user's owned ids exactly — partial
reorders would leave gaps in `position`.

title.description: Issue title (may be null in metadata) or the parsed number_raw fallback.

distinct_issues.description: Distinct issues from this series the user has read.

confirm_password.description:
Optional second password field. The web form ships two `<input>`s
for confirmation; submitting them as `new_password` + `confirm_password`
lets the no-JS fallback validate parity server-side. Ignored when
absent or equal.

revoked.description:
Number of sessions transitioned from active → revoked. Doesn't
include sessions that were already revoked or expired.

next_cursor.description:
`started_at` of the last row — caller passes back as `before=`
for the next page. `None` when no more rows.

SavedViewBatchReq.description: Request for the saved-view batch endpoint.

icon.description:
Per-user icon override key. `None` falls back to a kind-based
default resolved client-side. Free-form text — the client maps
it against its rail-icon registry and silently falls back if the
key is unknown.

is_system.description: True for system views (`user_id IS NULL`).

pinned.description: Whether the calling user has this view pinned to their home rail.

pinned_on_pages.description:
Multi-page rails M6: every page (system + custom) this view is
